test_cases.title: Complete checkout end-to-end shows the confirmation message
test_cases.description: Happy path: from a cart with a known item, the user enters first name, last name, and postal code, reaches the overview, clicks Finish, and sees the order confirmation message.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Checkout is a multi-page UI journey (cart -> information -> overview -> complete) on a front-end-only app; there is no backend order API to assert against, so the full flow and its final confirmation can only be validated end-to-end in the browser.
preconditions: Logged in as standard_user with one known product added to the cart (setup).

steps.action: Enter first name, last name, and postal code, then continue.
On the overview page, click Finish.

expected_results: The order completes and a confirmation message is shown (e.g. 'Thank you for your order').
The URL reflects the checkout-complete step.

test_cases.title: Overview page shows the correct item
test_cases.description: The item added to the cart appears on the checkout overview page by name (guards against missing/extra/substituted items at the confirm step).
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Whether the overview reflects the cart contents is a rendered-UI assertion mid-journey; with no order API to inspect, it must be checked end-to-end. P0 because confirming a wrong order is a direct money/trust failure (RISK-002).
preconditions: Logged in as standard_user with one known product (e.g. 'Sauce Labs Backpack') added to the cart (setup).

steps.action: From the cart, click Checkout and complete the information step with valid data.
On the overview page, read the listed item name(s).

expected_results: The overview lists exactly the product that was added to the cart (same name).
No unexpected extra items are present.

test_cases.title: Information step blocks a missing required field
test_cases.description: Submitting the checkout information step with a required field empty (e.g. postal code) is rejected with an error rather than proceeding to the overview.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The information form's required-field validation is enforced in the UI on this front-end-only app; there is no server endpoint to validate against, so the negative case is verified end-to-end (assert an error appears and the overview is not reached).
preconditions: Logged in as standard_user with one product in the cart; on the checkout information step (setup).

steps.action: Enter first name and last name but leave postal code empty, then click Continue.

expected_results: An error message is shown indicating a required field is missing.
The user remains on the information step and does not reach the overview page.
